hero.kicker: ASHLEY GORGE VIA FERRATA · VERNAL, UTAH
hero.headline: Show up. Gear Up. Climb.
hero.subhead: Self-serve via ferrata rentals in a locker steps from the trailhead. No shop stops. No detours. Just grab your gear and go!

whatIsViaFerrata.heading: What is a Via Ferrata?
whatIsViaFerrata.body: Via ferrata — Italian for 'iron path' — is a protected climbing route featuring steel cables, iron rungs, and suspension bridges bolted into the rock. It lets everyday adventurers experience real vertical terrain in relative safety, using a specialized harness and lanyard system that clips to a steel cable line as you climb. You don't need to be a technical climber, just fit, unafraid of heights, and equipped with the right gear.
whatIsViaFerrata.callout: Ashley Gorge is set to be the longest public via ferrata in the Western Hemisphere.

howItWorks.heading: Rentals Right at the Trailhead.
howItWorks.intro: Skip the hassle of driving in and out of town for your gear. Our smart lockers are conveniently located right at the trailhead.

steps.title: Reserve Your Gear
steps.body: Pick your kit online in minutes.

steps.title: Sign the Waiver
steps.body: Ours and Uintah County's — done digitally before you arrive.

steps.title: Watch the Safety Video
steps.body: A quick guide to gear use and course hazards.

steps.title: Pay and Grab Your PIN
steps.body: The locker opens with your unique code.

steps.title: Climb, Then Return
steps.body: Drop the gear back in the locker on your way out.

tips.title: Hydrate Hard
tips.body: Bring at least 2 liters of water per person. There's zero shade on most of the course.

tips.title: Wear Real Shoes
tips.body: Hiking shoes work; approach shoes are better. Grip matters.

tips.title: Bring Gloves
tips.body: Not required, but the metal gets hot in midday sun — most climbers wish they had them.

tips.title: Plan Your Day
tips.body: The full route runs 2–3 hours moving fast, 7–8 if you're taking your time or if the course is busy.

tips.title: Pack Snacks
tips.body: A protein bar or two goes a long way. There's nowhere to buy food nearby.

items.title: UIAA-Certified Gear
items.body: Every helmet, harness, and lanyard meets international climbing safety standards.

items.title: Fully Waivered
items.body: Ridgeline and Uintah County waivers required for all climbers.

items.title: BLM Permit Pending
items.body: Operated by Randall Ventures LLC under a Special Recreation Permit (in review).

gallery.heading: From the Gorge
gallery.intro: A look at the terrain, the bridges, and the people who've been out on the iron path.

contact.heading: Questions? Reach Out.
contact.body: We're happy to answer anything about gear, the route, or how our locker system works. The best way to get us is by phone or email.
